title: DOM Manipulation

questions.question_text: What is the modern way to attach a click event to an element in JS?
questions.explanation: addEventListener is the modern standard, keeping your HTML clean and allowing multiple listeners on one element.
The DOM is a programming interface for HTML and XML documents.

questions.question_text: Which method is the most efficient way to select a single element by its ID?
questions.explanation: getElementById is highly optimized as IDs must be unique in a document.

questions.question_text: How do you change the text content of an element?
questions.explanation: textContent is safer for plain text, while innerHTML allows HTML tags.

questions.question_text: Which method is used to listen for a user clicking a button?
questions.explanation: addEventListener is the standard way to attach event handlers.

questions.question_text: How do you add a CSS class 'active' to an element?
questions.explanation: classList provides convenient methods like add, remove, and toggle.

options: When an event disappears after being called
When an event fires on a child element and then moves up to its parents
When an event happens multiple times a second
When the browser reloads after an event
questions.explanation: Events propagate from the inner-most target up through the DOM tree.

questions.question_text: Which property would you use to change the background color of an element directly in JS?
questions.explanation: JS uses camelCase for CSS properties when using the .style object.

questions.question_text: How do you create a completely new <div> element using JS?
questions.explanation: This creates the element in memory; you then need to append it to the DOM.

questions.question_text: Which method adds a node as the last child of a parent element?
questions.explanation: appendChild is older; append is more modern and can handle multiple nodes or strings.

questions.question_text: How do you prevent a form from submitting (reloading the page) after a click?
questions.explanation: preventDefault stops the browser's default behavior for that event.

questions.question_text: What does a 'NodeList' returned by querySelectorAll differ from a normal 'Array'?
options: It's a collection of nodes that doesn't have all array methods like map() or filter() natively (though it has forEach)
It can only be used in CSS
questions.explanation: You often need to convert it to an array using Array.from() to use advanced methods.

questions.question_text: Which property refers to the parent of the current element?
questions.explanation: Both parentNode and parentElement traverse up the tree.

questions.question_text: How do you remove an element from the DOM?
questions.explanation: The modern .remove() method is the easiest way to delete an element.

questions.question_text: What does the 'value' property do on an <input> element?
options: Gets or sets the current text typed by the user
Sets the price of the item
Shows a popup with the text
questions.explanation: Accessing .value is how you process user input in forms.

questions.question_text: Which event fires when the entire HTML document has been fully loaded and parsed?
questions.explanation: DOMContentLoaded is the best time to start running JS that interacts with the DOM.
